content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master` with focus on the new `iterative_minimal` workflow. I found three fix-now issues: retry/resume state incorrectly spans stage attempts, resume preflight can block recovery of an already-finished terminal iteration, and the diff fingerprint now masks tracked changes under hard-coded build-artifact path names. Static review only; I did not run `cargo test`, `cargo clippy`, or `nix build`.

## Amendments

1. Finding: `iterative_minimal` carries `completed_iterations`/`stable_count` across stage retries, so a retryable failure can satisfy the stability stop condition too early. `carry_forward_iterative_state` only keys on stage/cycle/completion_round and ignores `attempt` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:741-749`), the retry path restores that state before `cursor.retry()` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5776-5854`), and resume reconstruction also stitches `ImplementerIterationCompleted` events together without any attempt boundary (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1162-1215`). In practice, one no-diff iteration, then a transport failure, then one more no-diff iteration on the next attempt will exit as `stable` even though there were not two consecutive successful implementer rounds in the retried attempt. Reset iterative loop state when `attempt` changes, and include attempt boundaries when rebuilding state from the journal.
   Rationale: This changes the core stop-condition semantics of the new flow: transient backend failures can make the loop terminate after too few successful no-diff rounds, which means final review can run against a workspace that was never actually shown to be stable under the configured threshold.
2. Finding: `run resume` still preflights the current implementer target before the code can recover a terminal iterative result from disk. `resume_run_with_retry_internal` unconditionally calls `preflight_check` on the remaining plan (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:2234-2243`), and preflight hard-fails on `check_availability` for non-panel stages (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:383-397`). But `resume_terminal_iterative_stage_result` can complete `plan_and_implement` from the persisted `itN.parsed.json` without re-invoking the implementer (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6172-6203`). If the implementer backend becomes unavailable after the loop already reached `stable`/`max_rounds` but before `stage_completed` is durably recorded, resume incorrectly fails instead of finalizing the already-produced result.
   Rationale: The new recovery path is meant to avoid reinvoking the implementer after a terminal loop has already finished. Requiring the current backend to be available before that recovery path runs defeats the feature in a realistic crash-recovery case and leaves the run unnecessarily stuck.
3. Finding: the new diff fingerprint unconditionally hides any top-level `target`, `target-final`, `result`, and `result-*` path from both git-based and filesystem-based change detection (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6207-6231`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6426-6466`). Because `ralph-burning` runs against arbitrary repositories, that means tracked project files/directories with those names are invisible to `iterative_minimal`; edits there will be treated as "no diff" and can prematurely increment the stability counter or exit the loop. Restrict the ignore list to untracked/ignored build artifacts, or make it configurable instead of blanket-excluding tracked paths by name.
   Rationale: This turns pathname conventions from this repo into global semantics for every repo the tool orchestrates. In repos that legitimately version those paths, the stabilization loop will silently miss real edits, which is a correctness bug in the new diff-detection logic.
